ucs.title: [LLM] Scattered Spider helpdesk-driven MFA/password reset followed by RMM tool stack deployment
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Scattered Spider (UNC3944 / Octo Tempest) chain: vishing the IT helpdesk to reset a privileged user's password or re-register MFA, signing in from a fresh device, and immediately dropping multiple legitimate RMM agents (AnyDesk, ScreenConnect, Splashtop, Pulseway, Fleetdeck, Ngrok) for redundant interactive C2 before lateral movement and cloud snapshot exfil.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as proc_names from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("anydesk.exe","teamviewer.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","splashtop.exe","SplashtopStreamer.exe","pulseway.exe","ngrok.exe","fleetdeck.exe","rustdesk.exe","meshagent.exe","Action1.exe","atera.exe","syncro.exe","level.exe","tacticalrmm.exe") by Processes.user Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval rmm_count=mvcount(proc_names) | join type=inner user [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as resetTime from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where (All_Changes.action IN ("password_reset","modified") AND All_Changes.object_category IN ("user","identity")) OR All_Changes.change_type IN ("AAD MFA Method Registered","StrongAuthenticationMethod","Reset user password") by All_Changes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Changes)` ] | where firstSeen >= resetTime AND firstSeen <= (resetTime + 43200) AND rmm_count >= 2 | table user dest resetTime firstSeen rmm_count proc_names cmds
ucs.kql:
let rmmTools = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","teamviewer.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","splashtop.exe","splashtopstreamer.exe","pulseway.exe","ngrok.exe","fleetdeck.exe","rustdesk.exe","meshagent.exe","action1.exe","atera.exe","syncro.exe","level.exe","tacticalrmm.exe"]);
let idChanges = CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(2d)
| where ActionType in ("Reset user password.","Change user password.","Update user.","User registered security info","User started security info registration","Admin reset user password.")
| extend TargetUpn = tolower(tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData.ObjectId))))
| extend TargetUpn = iff(isempty(TargetUpn), tolower(tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData.Target[0].ID)))), TargetUpn)
| project ChangeTime=Timestamp, TargetUpn, ChangeAction=ActionType, ChangeActor=AccountDisplayName, ChangeIP=IPAddress;
let rmmExec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(2d)
| where FileName in~ (rmmTools) or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ (rmmTools)
| project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn=tolower(AccountUpn), FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName;
rmmExec
| join kind=inner (idChanges) on $left.AccountUpn == $right.TargetUpn
| where ExecTime between (ChangeTime .. ChangeTime + 12h)
| summarize ToolsRun=make_set(FileName), ToolCount=dcount(FileName), CmdLines=make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10), arg_min(ExecTime, *) by AccountUpn, DeviceName, ChangeTime, ChangeAction
| where ToolCount >= 2 or ToolsRun has_any ("ngrok.exe","fleetdeck.exe")
ucs.rationale: Scattered Spider's Mandiant/CrowdStrike (UNC3944, Muddled Libra) and Microsoft (Octo Tempest) profiles all emphasize the same opening play: vish the service desk for an MFA/password reset on a high-privilege user, then deploy a redundant RMM stack rather than custom malware. The two-of-N RMM threshold within 12h of an identity reset is the specific behavioral fingerprint MGM/Caesars and 2023-2024

ucs.title: [LLM] Scattered Spider AAD federated domain backdoor (Set-DomainAuthentication / AADInternals)
ucs.description: Detects Scattered Spider's cloud persistence move where, after compromising a Global Admin via SIM-swap or helpdesk reset, the actor adds or modifies an Entra ID federated domain (often via AADInternals' ConvertTo-AADIntBackdoor or Set-MsolDomainAuthentication) so they can forge SAML tokens for any user. This is a known UNC3944/Octo Tempest persistence pattern flagged by Microsoft IR.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Changes.src) as src_ip values(All_Changes.user_agent) as ua values(All_Changes.result) as result from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where All_Changes.change_type IN ("AzureActiveDirectory","Entra") AND All_Changes.action IN ("Set domain authentication.","Set federation settings on domain.","Add unverified domain","Add verified domain","Update domain") by All_Changes.user All_Changes.object All_Changes.action | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Changes)` | join type=outer user [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as signinTime values(Authentication.src) as auth_src from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success AND Authentication.app IN ("Microsoft Graph","Azure Active Directory PowerShell","Microsoft Azure CLI","AADInternals") by Authentication.user Authentication.app | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` ] | eval risk=case(action="Set federation settings on domain.",100, action="Set domain authentication.",90, true(),60) | table firstTime user object action app src_ip auth_src ua risk | where risk>=90
ucs.kql:
let federationOps = dynamic(["Set domain authentication.","Set federation settings on domain.","Add unverified domain","Add verified domain","Update domain","Add partner to company."]);
let suspiciousApps = dynamic(["AADInternals","Microsoft Graph PowerShell","Azure Active Directory PowerShell","Microsoft Azure CLI","Microsoft Azure PowerShell"]);
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where ActionType in (federationOps)
| extend ModProps = tostring(RawEventData.ModifiedProperties)
| extend TargetDomain = tostring(RawEventData.Target[0].ID)
| project ChangeTime=Timestamp, Actor=AccountDisplayName, ActorUpn=AccountObjectId, ChangeAction=ActionType, TargetDomain, ModProps, IPAddress, UserAgent
| join kind=leftouter (
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where Application in (suspiciousApps) or ClientAppUsed in (suspiciousApps)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | project SignInTime=Timestamp, ActorUpn=AccountObjectId, SignInIP=IPAddress, SignInApp=Application, SignInCountry=Country, SignInUA=UserAgent
) on ActorUpn
| where isnull(SignInTime) or SignInTime between (ChangeTime - 6h .. ChangeTime + 1h)
| extend RiskScore = case(ChangeAction == "Set federation settings on domain.", 100, ChangeAction == "Set domain authentication.", 95, 70)
| project ChangeTime, Actor, ChangeAction, TargetDomain, IPAddress, UserAgent, SignInApp, SignInIP, SignInCountry, RiskScore, ModProps
| sort by ChangeTime desc
ucs.rationale: Federation tampering in Entra ID is operationally rare (most tenants never call Set-MsolDomainAuthentication after initial setup) but it is a documented Octo Tempest / UNC3944 persistence move per Microsoft DART and Mandiant reporting on 2023 hospitality and insurance intrusions. Pairing the audit event with a sign-in from AADInternals or Graph PowerShell from a non-corporate IP discriminates Scat
